Off Calendar Mar-22-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
Albert Lee, Esq. 415-215-6868 Plaintiff (co-counsel)
Jakrun Sodhi, Esq. 209-900-8200 Sodhi Law Group for Defendant.

THE PARTIES STIPULATE TO THE MATTER BEING DECIDED BY THE JUDGE PRO TEM. THE PARTIES AND THE JUDGE PRO TEM APPEARING REMOTELY AUTHORIZE THE CLERK TO SIGN THE FORMAL STIPULATION FORM ON THEIR BEHALF.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Plaintiff's Motion to Reopen Discovery and Request for Monetary Sanctions is GRANTED. CCP 2024.050(a) states, in relevant part, that, "[o]n motion of any party, the court may grant leave to reopen discovery after a new trial date has been set. This motion shall be accompanied by a meet and confer declaration under Section 2016.040." CCP 2024.050(b) allows the court to take into account, among other factors: (1) The necessity and the reasons for the discovery; (2) The diligence or lack of diligence of the party seeking the discovery or the hearing of a discovery motion; and (3) The reasons that the discovery was not completed or that the discovery motion was not heard earlier. The Motion is granted such that discovery is reopened for the limited purposes of (1) obtaining the production of documents that defendants did not produce in discovery but listed in their January 27, 2023 Trial Exhibit List; and (2) conducting the deposition of Graham Scott with production of documents. Plaintiff failed to argue or present any evidence on why a motion to compel discovery was not made within 45 days of the pre-trial discovery requests. All requests for sanctions are denied.

During oral argument, Defense counsel asked whether Plaintiff's counsel would stipulate to reopen discovery for both sides. Plaintiff's counsel would not stipulate.

Moving party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312 by noon today directly to the Judge Pro Tem at Peter.Catalanotti@wilsonelser.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Peter Catalanotti, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not reported. =(302/JPT)
